author: parithosh
content: <@358120958726373381> how do you do this in lighthouse?

author: arnetheduck
content:
one thing about deposit verification is that afair, you can make the all-happy case 2x faster via batch verification - however, what that means is that the unhappy case becomes 1.5x slower (since you first do a batch which fails, then individually check one-by-one to find exactly which deposits are invalid) .. 
-> this needs worst-case analysis and not just "how do implementations handle the happy case"

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah we plan to setup happy case and worst case

author: barnabasbusa
content: Just give us a way to verify how long does it take

author: parithosh
content: yeah we're struggling to get any data rn, but we will def test both paths. assertoor should be able to send some invalids at random to break batching.

author: arnetheduck
content: hm, well, for nimbus at least, it's pretty simple in the sense that it's linear in the number of deposits - we don't do batch because of said pessimizations in the worst case so it's basically one sig check per deposit no matter if it's valid or not - and how long this takes varies with hardware obviously, but it can be benchmarked by benching the bls library effectively

author: arnetheduck
content: since we all use blst effectively this comes down to re-running blst benchmarks

author: arnetheduck
content: ah - and the pubkey deserialization is part of that, ie deserializing the pubkey is also slow (because it requires an extra check vs a known-valid pubkey)

author: arnetheduck
content: wouldn't it be enough to generate ranges of blocks with and without deposits and compare?

author: barnabasbusa
content: is it possible to add a debug log message for printing out "it took xyz ms to process xyz deposits"

author: barnabasbusa
content: then we can grep for that

author: parithosh
content: even if on a branch, we only really need this data for now. but yeah, otherwise we have to rely on slot timings, but that's mostly event stream collected info and the performance there depends on event stream implementations by clients (ie when they emit them)

author: parithosh
content: it wont be an apples to apples comparison

author: arnetheduck
content: I was about to add a log like that but just looking at the code, I see it needs optimizing first, or nimbus will be overly pessimistic for your purposes :/ might take a bit for us

author: 0xunclebill
content: We don’t have anything yet ill add a trace or metric for this

author: terencechain
content: i added a pre processing cache and batch verify them in size 8, i think we can do up to 16k easily, but we still need to test the unhappy case and find a bound on how much bad sigs we can support before getting reorg
